content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration in autonomous agents can be adaptively bounded by the agent's working memory capacity limit, creating a self-regulating exploration-exploitation loop that avoids catastrophic information overload. Instead of rewarding all novel state discovery equally, ICM can be modified to only generate intrinsic rewards for new explorations that fit within the agent's current available working memory space.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
